source_title: Terracotta Is the Biggest Kitchen Cabinet Color for 2026 ? But They?re Not ?Too Bold or Trend-Driven? Say Interior Designers
caption: Terracotta is having its moment in 2026 kitchens, and the interesting thing is it never really left...

source_title: 5 of the Best Farrow & Ball Colors for a Bathroom That Designers Turn to Time and Time Again
caption:
The myth: bathroom walls have to be white or off-white to look clean. It is a piece of conventional wisdom that has kept a lot of beautiful rooms looking like clinic waiting areas.

Bathrooms are actually one of the better rooms to commit to a real color. The space is small, the light is controlled, and you are in there at specific moments of the day. A deep slate on the walls at eye level, a dusty sage that shifts between morning light and candlelight, a soft charcoal above white tile, these work. They can work in a compact 6x8 bathroom in Ukhrul the same way they work in the Farrow & Ball mood boards designers reference.

The rule is not "go light." The rule is "go intentional."

Save this before your next bathroom repaint, or share it with someone who needs permission to go darker.

source_title: Miami Vice Meets Art Deco Design in this Paris Penthouse
caption:
When a client brings in a bold, layered, high-contrast reference like this, the most useful conversation is not about which color to pick. It is about what that kind of finish commitment actually costs to maintain.

Decorative and high-gloss finishes on feature walls show everyday wear more visibly than matte emulsions. In a tropical highland climate like Ukhrul's, multi-layer finishes need recoating sooner than the standard guidance suggests. And the cost difference between a straightforward repaint and a proper decorative finish build-up is significant enough to factor in from day one.

Bold interiors are completely achievable here. But the look and the long-term plan have to be designed together. MAK-SHANG always walks through care and cost before the first tin is opened.

Save this if you are planning a statement room and want to go in with a clear picture.
